channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Notable Quotes from Henry David Thoreau Highlighted in New Documentary
item.description: A new three-part PBS documentary brings renewed attention to the 19th-century writer and naturalist Henry David Thoreau. Featuring a star-studded cast including Jeff Goldblum as Thoreau and narration ...

item.title: The Challenges of Excelling in Test Cricket Compared to T20
item.description: Ramachandra Guha reflects on the challenges of being a successful Test cricketer versus excelling in T20 cricket. He draws on Scyld Berry's book, '500 Declared', which chronicles the experience of cov...

item.title: Pakistan clarifies UAE financial deposits return is routine transaction
item.description: On Saturday, the Foreign Office (FO) clarified that reports suggesting any controversy around financial deposits from the United Arab Emirates (UAE) were misleading and unfounded. A senior Pakistani o...

item.title: Fiscal Pressure, Health Systems, and Local Government Responsibilities in the Philippines
item.description: The article examines how historical debt crises in the Philippines, particularly during the early 1980s under the Marcos administration, directly impacted public health systems. Despite apparent incre...

item.title: DCI Explains Method for Valuing Seized Narcotics in Kenya
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI), through its Anti-Narcotics Unit (ANU), has clarified the step-by-step process it uses to determine the street value of drugs recovered in operations a...

item.title: Collection of 100 Easter Greetings, Wishes and Prayers for 2026
item.description: Good morning Naija! Naija News don bring una 100 correct Easter messages, prayers, and wishes wey you fit send to your family, friends, and loved ones dis Easter 2026. Dis collection get plenty messag...

item.title: Coalition faces challenges over One Nation’s support for Trump and MAGA movement
item.description: The article discusses the potential political implications for the Coalition if it chooses to emphasise One Nation leader Pauline Hanson’s support for Donald Trump and the MAGA movement. Liberal MP An...

item.title: Sweden’s Niklas Edin Secures Eighth World Curling Title with 9-6 Win over Canada
item.description: Sweden’s Niklas Edin captured his eighth men’s world curling championship with a 9-6 victory over Canada’s Matt Dunstone in Ogden, Utah. Despite a challenging showing at the February Olympic Games, wh...

item.title: Festival of Chariots brings culture and colour to Durban beachfront
item.description: The Festival of Chariots, also known as Ratha-Yatra, is currently taking place at the Bay of Plenty lawns along the Durban beachfront, running until Monday. Organised by the International Society for ...

item.title: Artemis II Mission and White House Turmoil Highlight Unusual Week
item.description: This week has been extraordinary both in space and on Earth. The Artemis II mission marked the first human travel beyond Earth's orbit since 1972, with astronauts describing the view of Earth as 'spec...

item.title: Atiku condemns Wike’s threat to journalist as dangerous and unacceptable
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar don come out strongly against the statement wey Nyesom Wike, Minister of the FCT, make against journalist Seun Okinbaloye. Atiku talk say e dey disgraceful and dan...

item.title: Trump sets 48-hour deadline for Iran amid escalating Middle East conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has issued a stern warning to Iran, giving Tehran 48 hours to reach a deal or face severe consequences, as tensions in the Middle East escalate. This latest ultimatum follows...

item.title: Holy Saturday Sermon: Reflections on Easter and Faith
item.description: For dis Holy Saturday, Bishop Matthew Hassan Kukah for Sokoto celebrate di climax of di Christian Lenten journey. Him sermon mark di resurrection of Jesus Christ, di night wey end all fear, darkness, ...

item.title: Easter holiday bookings remain largely stable despite record fuel prices
item.description: Despite record-high petrol prices, the anticipated surge in Easter holiday booking cancellations has not materialised. NRMA, which operates roughly 50 holiday parks and resorts across Australia, repor...

item.title: Filipinos Head Back to Metro Manila and Provinces After Holy Week
item.description: After the three-day Holy Week break, Filipinos have started returning to their workplaces and homes on Easter Sunday. In key bus terminals across Metro Manila, such as Cubao in Quezon City and Rizal A...

item.title: Four people die and toddler critically injured in multiple road accidents across Queensland
item.description: Queensland experienced a tragic night of multiple road accidents resulting in four deaths and one critically injured toddler. Around 9pm on Saturday, a head-on collision occurred on School Road in Log...

item.title: Yanga win big against Prisons to strengthen top position in NBC Premier League
item.description: Dar es Salaam. Young Africans SC, popularly known as Yanga, bounced back strongly in the NBC Premier League by defeating Tanzania Prisons 3-0 at KMC Complex. This win ended their recent three-match dr...

item.title: Occasional Heavy Drinking Significantly Increases Liver Fibrosis Risk in Adults with MASLD
item.description: A recent study from Keck Medicine of USC highlights that episodic heavy drinking—even just once a month—substantially increases the risk of advanced liver scarring, particularly in individuals with me...

item.title: Reflections on Leadership and Development in Murang’a County
item.description: Governor Irungu Kang'ata of Murang’a County compares the journey of governorship to pursuing a PhD, where learning never really ends and practical challenges keep coming. He emphasises that winning an...

item.title: Economic hardships hit paraffin-dependent households while officials increase their salaries
item.description: While vehicle owners recently received some relief on fuel prices, the poorest South Africans who rely on paraffin are facing doubled costs, from R11.67 to R23.19 per litre. Government cushioning meas...
